content.problemStatement:
An array of size MMM is called **good**, if, for each pair of integers (i,j)(i, j)(i,j) (1‚Č§i<j‚Č§M)(1 \leq i \lt j \leq M)(1‚Č§i<j‚Č§M):


Ai‚ą£Aj=(Ai‚äēAj)+AjA_i | A_j = (A_i\oplus A_j) + A_jAi‚Äč‚ą£Aj‚Äč=(Ai‚Äč‚äēAj‚Äč)+Aj‚Äč, where ‚ą£|‚ą£ denotes bitwise OR operator and ‚äē\oplus‚äē denotes bitwise XOR operator.
Note that an array having a single element (M=1)(M = 1)(M=1) is also **good**.

You are given a permutation PPP of size NNN. Find the number of **good** subsequences of the given permutation. Since the answer can be huge, print it modulo 109+710^9+7109+7.

A permutation of size NNN contains each integer from 111 to NNN exactly once.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
The first line of each test case contains an integer NNN - the length of the permutation PPP.
The second line of each test case contains NNN space-separated integers P1,P2,‚Ä¶,PNP_1,P_2,\ldots,P_NP1‚Äč,P2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,PN‚Äč.


Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line, the number of good subsequences of PPP, modulo 109+710^9 + 7109+7.
constraints: PPP is guaranteed to be a permutation.
The sum of NNN over all test cases won't exceed 2‚čÖ1052 \cdot 10^52‚čÖ105.
